headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	At first we found life in the town very **exciting** but soon it became rather ......................
2.	By working hard, Benjamin **outshone** his elder brother instead of being ....................
3.	When we were busy discussing **weighty** matters, James introduced a ................ issue.
4.	The delegates found it better to resort to **consultation** rather than .................
5.	This house which was **sealed off** years ago has now been ....................
6.	The government has decided that, from next year military service will no longer be **compulsory**: it will be ................
7.	**Co-operating** with teachers promotes learning, it does not help to be .....................
8.	Only two months after the honour was **conferred** on the Minister, his dishonesty made the Head of State ................ it
9.	Despite all my attempts to **refute** the charges against me, my sister tried to ..................... them.
10.	The government's efforts to **improve** the economy of the country rather ...................... it.

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences.

S2.qs: 11.	I really like working in that office; I found the ................... very friendly
12.	Our teacher's main aim was to ......................... us with his good work
13.	Problems in the city have multiplied with the .............. of people from the rural areas
14.	It was so hot in the house that the boys rushed to the beach to enjoy the ..................
15.	The thief ran so fast that my attempt to get him was in ....................
16.	The hungry refugees had to queue for their ....................
17.	This land is very dry; we have to ................. the plants
18.	It is not right for you to insult your parents; you will just ................. trouble for yourself.
19.	The meeting of the warring factions ended on a ............... note
20.	We cannot tell which of the historian’s	................... is true

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	Now that Michael has become rich, Nancy has begun to make much of him. This means that Nancy

opts.q1: B. now gets a lot of money from Michael
opts.q3: D. no longer wants to leave Michael

S3.qs: 22.	We wanted to arrive early so we decided to leave at 10 o’clock at the latest. This means that we

opts.q0: A. must leave at 10 o'clock exactly
opts.q1: B. should not leave later than 10 o’clock
opts.q2: C. should not leave before 10 o’clock

S3.qs: 23.	My brother's drunkenness often got on my nerves. This means that my brother’s drunkenness
24.	The government has decided to freeze student grants. This means that the government intends to

opts.q0: A. reduce grants meant for students
opts.q1: B. resume paying grants to students
opts.q2: C. make students repay the grants
opts.q3: D. stop paying grants to students

S3.qs: 25.	The first time Paul met Ngozi, her beauty caught his eye. This means that
26.	Mr. Juma kept his class on their toes so that they might not fail the examination. This means that Mr. Juma made his pupils

opts.q0: A. work very hard to pass

S3.qs: 27.	The President did everything he could not to lose face with Parliament. This means that the President tried to

opts.q2: C. win the respect of the people
opts.q3: D. gain more seats in the next elections

S3.qs: 28.	Drought, for the first time, brought the poor farmers face-to-face with famine. This means that the farmers had never

opts.q0: A. expected to meet hungry people
opts.q1: B. thought the season would be dry
opts.q3: D. experienced hunger in their lives

S3.qs: 29.	You can be sure that Sule will hold his own in this dispute. This means that Sule will

opts.q0: A. claim victory in the case
opts.q1: B. insist that he owns the property
opts.q2: C. maintain that his opinion is right
opts.q3: D. prove that he won the argument

S3.qs: 30.	The captain must have been really ill to ask the coach not to include him in the team. This means that the

opts.q0: A. captain demanded not to be excluded by the coach
opts.q1: B. coach would not have dropped the captain
opts.q2: C. coach had decided to exclude the captain
opts.q3: D. captain requested to be dropped from the team

S4.headd: From the words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the sentence

S4.qs: 31.	It was **callous** of you to have turned down the poor man’s request.
32.	After a long walk, the travellers suddenly saw lights **blazing** ahead
33.	John said nothing during the meeting; he just sat there **fuming**.
34.	The employees hated him because he often **abused** his authority
35.	The minister **abridged** his speech since he had to leave early
36.	For rescuing the boy, the policewoman was promoted for her **gallantry**
37.	Nora's **goal** was to become a doctor
38.	Our staff was very proud when the school **clinched** all the prizes
39.	Mamie complains that her husband often **relegates** her to the position of a housemaid
40.	There is no **vacancy**, so the company no longer recruits staff.

S5.headd: From the word or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 41.	The police are committed to ............... safety of citizens.
42.	No sooner had the thief seen the policemen ................ he escaped.
43.	Our teacher kept us kneeling ............... the whole period.
44.	I know you now; I .................. that you could be trusted.
45.	The chief, togetehr with his elders ................ welcome to the durbar.
46.	Our car broke down so we had to go ................. foot.
47.	Funke's mother prefers Jane ..................... Ama.
48.	You haven't shown any interest ................... providing for yourself.
49.	All workers are expected to be answerable .................. their bosses.
50.	The hall was virtually empty; we had expected one hundred people at the very ......................
51.	We did not have enough room so daddy turned the garage ...................... an additional bedroom
52.	I was looking forward to .................. from you.
53.	Most governments sink large sums of money .................. developing their countries
54.	It was on Wednesday that we met in Accra ................... ?
55.	We have waited for too long; its high time we .....................
56.	I don’t believe Banjul is far from here; it must be ten kilometres, .......................
57.	You should not blame me; it is no fault of ....................

59.	The coffee is still bitter; you have not added ................... sugar.
60.	You haven't seen them for a long time, ....................... ?
61.	................. taxes is a civic responsibility
62.	John and James are good friends; they are very fond of ...................
63.	No wonder Pabi is now in the national team; he ................. very well since his youth
64.	You may hate Mathematics but you must stick .................... it if you intend to study Physics.
65.	I never knew I could be so fast ................. I entered the competition.
66.	The students are complaining ................... management’s high-handedness.
67.	My aunt said that she would not buy me ........................ of those two books for school.
68.	I am quite sure that I did not see ................ in the classroom last night.
69.	I pointed to an object in the sky, but they ..................... it.
70.	..................... average, 1000 vehicles cross this bridge daily
71.	A dictator often lives ................... fear of assassination.
72.	Jumai lives in a small street ...................... Murtala Muhammed Way
73.	I impressed ................... him the necessity of his going overseas
